title: Unit 4: Divide and Conquer
description: Solve sorting tasks by partitioning datasets recursively, enabling O(n log n) efficiency.

topics.title: 4.1 Quick Sort
topics.description: Picks a pivot, partitions elements around pivot (smaller left, larger right), and applies quicksort recursively to sub-arrays.

complexity.time: O(n log n) average, O(n^2) worst case

ispo.desc: Receives array low and high limits.
Partition routine receives the bounds.
Stores partitioning pivot index.
Declares pivot value and index pointer i.
Declares temp variable inside loop.
Declares temp variable outside loop.
Drives recursive divide and conquer branching.
Loops, shifting smaller elements left.
Swaps pivot element to sorted boundary.
Returns index of pivot partition.

quiz.q: What is the partition method's goal in Quick Sort?
o: To place the pivot in its correct sorted position and group smaller elements to the left, larger to the right
To split array into two equal halves

topics.title: 4.2 Merge Sort
topics.description: Recursively split array into halves, sort each half, and merge sorted halves back together.

ispo.desc: Accepts boundaries l and r.
Allocates midpoint 'm'.
Allocates auxiliary arrays L and R in memory.
Recursively splits array and calls merge function.
Iterates and merges items from temporary arrays back to original array in sorted order.

quiz.q: Is Merge Sort a stable sorting algorithm (does it preserve original order of duplicates)?

topics.title: 4.3 Heap Sort
topics.description: Convert the array to a Max-Heap binary tree representation, then repeatedly remove the maximum root and heapify to sort.

ispo.desc: Accepts array reference.
Allocates array size tracker 'n'.
Creates temporary swap variable 'temp'.
Creates largest node tracker, left child 'l' and right child 'r' pointers.
Builds max heap from unsorted array.
Extracts max element to end and calls heapify recursively to balance remaining heap.

quiz.q: What is the worst-case space complexity of Heap Sort?

topics.title: 4.4 Radix Sort
topics.description: Sort numbers digit by digit starting from least significant digit (LSD) to most significant digit (MSD) using counting sort as a subroutine.

complexity.time: O(d * (n + k)) where d is digits, k is base

ispo.desc: Accepts array reference.
Stores size of array n.
Stores candidate max value to find number of digits.
Allocates output buffer array and count frequency array.
Finds max value in array.
Iterates exponent value by multiplying by 10 to extract digits sequentially.
Counting sort logic: extracts digit place using division/modulo, computes prefix sum, and constructs sorted output array.

quiz.q: What stable sorting algorithm is typically used as a subroutine in Radix Sort?

topics.title: 4.5 Shell Sort
topics.description: An extension of insertion sort that allows swapping of far apart elements. The distance between elements is progressively reduced until it becomes 1.

complexity.time: O(n^2) worst case, O(n log n) best case

ispo.desc: Accepts array reference.
Stores size of array n.
Allocates temporary variable temp and index variable j.
Iterates gaps, performs gapped insertion comparisons, and shifts elements by gap width.

quiz.q: What is the initial gap size usually chosen in Shell Sort?
